The active code windows include artifacts.rs, we have expanded file symbols earlier, but still maybe need more evidence. The strategy wants targeted lexical read only when needed. We need to decide next step: maybe hydrate source for artifacts.rs? Actually recommended frontier actions include hydrate_source and expand_file. Expand_file already done maybe, but we can still hydrate_source to get source code. The current focus is artifacts.rs visible nodes 38 hidden unreachable 0 omitted symbols 217. recommended frontier actions: hydrate_source [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=0 priority=120 via session.hydrate(...). So we should call session.hydrate with target selector? The target selector likely is the file path: crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs. So we should make a tool_call session.hydrate@v1 with payload target selector maybe "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Then we will get the source. Then we can maybe get readiness state after hydrate.

Thus kind: tool_call, tool_call tool_name: session.hydrate, payload includes target selector. Also acquired_fact_ids maybe empty? We can leave empty array. readiness_state after tool call maybe "evidence_acquired"? But since we are about to call tool, we are not ready yet. The readiness_state likely "evidence_visible" after reading? But the spec says response contract: kind can be think|tool_call|complete. When kind is tool_call, we must include tool_call and fields acquired_fact_ids and readiness_state. Probably readiness_state is "evidence_visible"? Actually evidence_visible indicates evidence is visible but not yet acquired? Let's see earlier examples: they used readiness_state values: not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit. So before tool call, maybe "evidence_visible"? But we haven't read yet. The instruction: "Rereads are allowed, but prefer already visible evidence when possible." We are about to read source, so evidence_visible maybe after hydration? Hmm.

Better to set readiness_state as "evidence_visible" maybe indicating we are about to fetch evidence. Or maybe "not_ready". The prior history shows they used "evidence_visible" after a tool_result? Not sure. I think after tool_call we can set readiness_state to "evidence_visible" indicating we have evidence visible after hydration? But actually after hydration we will get source, but we haven't yet. The readiness_state likely describes state of evidence after this step. Could be "evidence_acquired" after acquiring fact ids? Not yet. Maybe "not_ready" is safe.

Thus produce:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Hydrating source for artifacts.rs to capture schema version definitions",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.hydrate@v1","payload":{"target":"crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Make sure JSON only, no extra fields. Ensure proper quoting. No trailing commas.
